goal: PDF reports: lineage traceability report and reconciliation summary report

acceptance_criteria.description: Lineage report data assembler: BuildLineageReport(db, engagementID, batchID) assembles a LineageReport struct containing: engagement metadata (name, source_system, schema_version), batch metadata (batch_id, status, row_count), summary stats (from db.GetLineageSummary — total_records, unique_members, fields_covered, transformation_types, exception_count), and per-handler grouped transformations (handler_name → []LineageEntry with column_name, source_value, result_value, member_id). Groups are sorted by handler_name, then column_name. Maximum 500 lineage entries per handler group (truncated with count indicator). Exported from api/report_handler.go alongside existing BuildMappingSpec.

HTML template for lineage report in report/templates/lineage_report.html using Go html/template (all values escaped). Includes: title page (engagement name, batch ID, generation date), executive summary section (total records, unique members, field count, exception count), per-handler sections with transformation tables (source_value → result_value, grouped by column), exception summary section. Professional styling matching M07a mapping spec template (same CSS framework, alternating row colors, consistent typography). Template rendered via report.RenderLineageReportHTML(data LineageReport) (string, error).

GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/reports/lineage/{batch_id}/pdf returns the lineage report as a PDF download for a specific batch. Content-Type: application/pdf. Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=lineage-{batch-id}-{date}.pdf. Uses BuildLineageReport + HTML template + Renderer interface (same pattern as mapping spec PDF). Returns 404 if engagement or batch not found. Returns 404 if batch has no lineage records. 25-second chromedp timeout (matching M07a). Requires authenticated user.

Reconciliation report data assembler: BuildReconciliationReport(db, engagementID) assembles a ReconciliationReport struct containing: engagement metadata, reconciliation summary (from db.GetReconciliationSummary — total, match, minor, major, error counts, gate_score, p1_count, tier scores), per-tier breakdown (from db.GetReconciliationByTier for tiers 1-3 — member_id, calc_name, legacy_value, recomputed_value, canonical_value, category, priority), and pattern analysis (from db.GetReconciliationPatterns — suspected_domain, direction, mean_variance, affected_members, correction info). Maximum 200 records per tier (truncated with count indicator).

HTML template for reconciliation report in report/templates/reconciliation_report.html. Includes: title page, executive summary with gate score gauge (green >= 0.95, yellow 0.80-0.94, red < 0.80), tier score breakdown table, match/mismatch distribution (total, match%, minor%, major%, error%), per-tier detail tables with color-coded priority (P1=red, P2=orange, P3=yellow), pattern analysis section with variance charts described as text tables (no JS charting in PDF). Same CSS framework as mapping spec and lineage templates.

GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/reports/reconciliation/pdf returns the reconciliation summary as a PDF download. Content-Type: application/pdf. Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=reconciliation-{sanitized-engagement-name}-{date}.pdf. Returns 404 if engagement not found or has no reconciliation data. 25-second chromedp timeout. Requires authenticated user. Large engagement guard: if total reconciliation records exceed 10,000, return 422 suggesting use of filtered JSON export instead.

critical: Data assemblers use existing DB query functions (GetLineageSummary, GetReconciliationSummary, GetReconciliationByTier, GetReconciliationPatterns) — no new DB queries needed for the data
HTML templates use html/template with automatic escaping — no XSS via member IDs or field values
Renderer interface used (not concrete BrowserPool) — tests mock without Chrome
Content-Disposition filename sanitized (same pattern as M07a)
Reports go through auth middleware — no public access
Truncation limits prevent unbounded report generation (500 lineage entries per group, 200 recon records per tier)
high: Templates share CSS framework with mapping spec template — consistent professional look
Tests use -short mode with mock Renderer — no Chrome binary needed in CI
Error paths tested: no data returns 404, timeout returns 504, too large returns 422
Gate score color-coding consistent with frontend (green >= 0.95, yellow >= 0.80, red < 0.80)
Lineage report is per-batch (not per-engagement) — consistent with lineage data model
medium: Pattern analysis section uses text tables, not embedded charts (PDF doesn't support interactive JS)
Report caching deferred to future sprint — regenerated on every request
PDF document title metadata set for accessibility
